title: Add warning if unpacked array bound positive index is converted to negative
body:
alu_tb.sv: ```systemverilog module alu_tb (input clk); logic [31:0] mem [32'h8000_0000 : 32'h8000_0000 + 2**16]; //logic [31:0] mem [2**32]; initial begin $readmemh("my.hex", mem); for(int i=0; i<20; ++i) $display("%d: %h", i, mem[32'h8000_0000+i]); $finish; end endmodule ``` my.hex: ``` @80000000 B7C0EDFE 9380D0EA 37E176FF 130161F5 ``` `tb.cpp`: 

#define vIncFile3(x) #x
#define vIncFile2(x) vIncFile3(V##x.h)
#define vIncFile(x) vIncFile2(x)
#include vIncFile(TOP)

#define VTOP3(x) V##x
#define VTOP2(x) VTOP3(x)
#define VTOP VTOP2(TOP)

#include "verilated.h"

int main(int argc, char** argv) {
	VerilatedContext context;
	context.traceEverOn(true);  // Enable signal dump generation
	context.commandArgs(argc, argv);  // Forward the arguments to the Verilated model
	VTOP top(&context);  // Instantiate the model

	top.clk = 0;
	while (!context.gotFinish()) {
			context.timeInc(1);  // Increment the time
			top.clk = !top.clk;
			top.eval();  // Evaluate the model
	}
}
Compiling & running ```sh verilator \ --top-module alu_tb \ --assert \ --cc \ --exe \ --build \ --trace-fst \ -j \ -CFLAGS -DTOP=alu_tb \ alu_tb.sv tb.cpp obj_dir/Valu_tb --verbose ``` outputs: ``` %Error: my.hex:1: $readmem file address beyond bounds of array Aborting... zsh: IOT instruction (core dumped) obj_dir/Valu_tb --verbose ``` The index `0x8000_0000` seems to cause the problem. If I reduce it to `0x800_0000` in both `my.hex` and `alu_tb.sv`, code works fine. I have the hunch that the array indexes are integers, so the range is -2^31 to 2^31-1. I would expect that Verilator outputs at least a warning during compilation. Verilator 4.228 2022-10-01. I did not try with the latest. On Arch Linux May we assist you in trying to fix this in Verilator yourself? Yes
